channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Study Reveals How Mosquitoes Use Visual Cues and Carbon Dioxide to Target Humans
item.description: New research from MIT and Georgia Tech has shed light on how mosquitoes locate human targets. By analyzing over 53 million data points collected from 20 experiments, scientists have created a dynamic ...

item.title: Goalkeeping errors cost Orlando Pirates and Richards Bay in 2–2 draw
item.description: The Betway Premiership clash between Orlando Pirates and Richards Bay at the Umhlathuze Sports Complex resulted in a nervy 2–2 draw dominated by two shaky goalkeeping performances. Orlando Pirates’ Si...

item.title: Bihar Road Accident: Bus and Truck Collision Claims 13 Lives
item.description: A terribly sad incident unfolded in Katihar district, Bihar, on Saturday evening. A bus, a truck, and a pickup vehicle were involved in a major collision on NH-31 in Kodha block, leaving at least 13 p...

item.title: North Bay Man Charged in Connection with Deaths of Four Kittens
item.description: A North Bay, Ontario man is facing animal cruelty charges following the tragic discovery of four deceased kittens. Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) responded to a call regarding the situation and subse...

item.title: Alcaraz and Sinner Set for Monte Carlo Title and No. 1 Battle
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz don reach another Monte Carlo Masters final after he beat local favourite Valentin Vacherot 6-4, 6-4 for semi-final. The young Spaniard dey aim to defend his title and also hold his wor...

item.title: VIT Chennai professor encourages students to pick courses aligned with their passions and goals
item.description: During The Hindu Education Plus Career Counselling Fair’26 held in Chennai, Professor T. Thyagarajan, Pro-Vice-Chancellor of VIT Chennai, spoke to students about the crucial role of personal interests...

item.title: Man arrested after attacking U.S. military aircraft at Shannon Airport
item.description: A man in his 40s was arrested at Shannon Airport in County Clare, Ireland, after allegedly climbing onto the wing of a U.S. Air Force C-130 Hercules transport aircraft and attacking it with a hatchet....

item.title: Ottawa police charge man with carrying replica firearm after University of Ottawa lockdown
item.description: Ottawa police have charged a man following Friday’s lockdown at the University of Ottawa. The incident began around 4:20 p.m. near the uOttawa LRT station when the man was seen carrying what appeared ...

item.title: Jannik Sinner Books Monte Carlo Final Spot After Defeating Zverev
item.description: World number two Jannik Sinner don show another level as he qualify reach di Monte Carlo Masters final for di first time. Di 24-year-old Italian beat Alexander Zverev cleanly 6-1, 6-4 for semifinal on...

item.title: Ian Baker-Finch questions LIV Golf players’ preparation for challenging majors
item.description: Former British Open champion Ian Baker-Finch has raised concerns over whether LIV Golf players are being adequately prepared for the demands of major tournaments. After half of LIV’s contingent missed...

item.title: Rory McIlroy loses ground at Augusta as rivals narrow margin during Masters third round
item.description: Rory McIlroy’s commanding six-shot lead at the Masters was gradually eroded during the third round at Augusta National. Starting the day as the defending champion and strong favourite, McIlroy struggl...

item.title: Persistent Difficulties in Daily Activities May Signal Early Alzheimer’s
item.description: For many older adults, day-to-day routines like making breakfast, paying bills, shopping, driving, and managing appointments are performed almost automatically. However, small disruptions in these rou...

item.title: AFAN Lagos Hails FG's Approval of 57 Improved Crop Varieties for Better Yields
item.description: Di All Farmers Association of Nigeria (AFAN), Lagos State Chapter, don commend di Federal Government well well for approving 57 new improved crop varieties to boost agricultural productivity and food ...

item.title: DA faces internal leadership vote and constitutional reforms
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) is heading into a crucial elective congress this weekend, where delegates will decide on 58 proposed amendments that could significantly reshape the party’s structure and ...

item.title: Gilas Women Secure Spot in FIBA 3x3 World Cup Qualifiers Knockout Round
item.description: The Gilas Pilipinas Women’s 3x3 basketball team has successfully advanced to the knockout stage of the FIBA 3x3 World Cup qualifiers. This achievement highlights their strong performance in the earlie...

item.title: Harambee Starlets Qualify for FIFA Series Final After Beating India
item.description: Kenya's women's national football team, Harambee Starlets, booked their place in the final of the 2026 FIFA Series after a solid 2-0 win over India at Nyayo National Stadium on Saturday evening. Despi...

item.title: Opposition Presses for Interior PS Omollo to Resign Amid Allegations of Political Violence
item.description: Interior Principal Secretary Raymond Omollo faces mounting pressure to resign from opposition leaders, who accuse him of orchestrating attacks on politicians critical of the current government. During...

item.title: PNP-IAS investigates PNPA hazing incident injuring 22 cadets
item.description: The Philippine National Police-Internal Affairs Service (PNP-IAS) has launched a thorough investigation following a reported hazing incident at the Philippine National Police Academy (PNPA) that injur...

item.title: Government hosts ceremony to honour Chris Hani in Mpumalanga
item.description: The South African government, through the Department of Sport, Arts and Culture in collaboration with the Mpumalanga provincial government, is hosting a commemoration of the late liberation struggle h...

item.title: Landmark Scottish ruling holds abuser responsible for partner’s suicide after prolonged domestic violence
item.description: In July 2023, twenty-eight-year-old Kimberly Milne took her own life by jumping from a bridge in Dundee after enduring months of domestic abuse from her estranged husband, Lee Milne. Her death led to ...
